name: Tafsir al-Mizan
author: Allamah Muhammad Husayn Tabatabai
text:
# Ayahs

```arabic
يَـٰٓأَيُّهَا ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟ لَا تُلْهِكُمْ أَمْوَٰلُكُمْ وَلَآ أَوْلَـٰدُكُمْ عَن ذِكْرِ ٱللَّهِ ۚ وَمَن يَفْعَلْ ذَٰلِكَ فَأُو۟لَـٰٓئِكَ هُمُ ٱلْخَـٰسِرُونَ (٩)
```

```english
O you who have faith! Do not let your possessions and children distract you from the remembrance of Allah, and whoever does that—it is they who are the losers. (9)
```

```arabic
وَأَنفِقُوا۟ مِن مَّا رَزَقْنَـٰكُم مِّن قَبْلِ أَن يَأْتِىَ أَحَدَكُمُ ٱلْمَوْتُ فَيَقُولَ رَبِّ لَوْلَآ أَخَّرْتَنِىٓ إِلَىٰٓ أَجَلٍ قَرِيبٍ فَأَصَّدَّقَ وَأَكُن مِّنَ ٱلصَّـٰلِحِينَ (١٠)
```

```english
Spend out of what We have provided you before death comes to any of you, whereat he might say, ‘My Lord, why did You not respite me for a short time so that I could give charity and become one of the righteous!’ (10)
```

```arabic
وَلَن يُؤَخِّرَ ٱللَّهُ نَفْسًا إِذَا جَآءَ أَجَلُهَا ۚ وَٱللَّهُ خَبِيرٌۢ بِمَا تَعْمَلُونَ (١١)
```

```english
But Allah will never respite anyone when his time has come, and Allah is well aware of what you do. (11)
```

## (Commentary)

This is an admonition to the believers (المؤمنين) to avoid certain traits that breed hypocrisy (النفاق), namely being diverted by wealth and children, and parsimony (البخل).

**His saying, the Exalted:** **يا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا لا تُلْهِكُمْ أَمْوالُكُمْ وَلا أَوْلادُكُمْ عَنْ ذِكْرِ اللهِ** "O you who have faith! Do not let your possessions and your children distract you from the remembrance of Allah" (63:9) etc. Distraction (الإلهاء) means preoccupation (الإشغال). What is meant by wealth and children distracting from the remembrance of Allah (ذكر الله) is their occupying the heart with attachment to them, such that it necessitates turning away from orienting oneself toward Allah, insofar as they are the "adornment of the life of this world" (زينة الحياة الدنيا). The Exalted said: **الْمالُ وَالْبَنُونَ زِينَةُ الْحَياةِ الدُّنْيا** "Wealth and children are an adornment of the life of the world" (18:46). Preoccupation with them causes the heart to be void of the remembrance of Allah and leads to forgetting Him, the Exalted; thus, nothing remains for the person except speech without action or heart-felt confirmation (تصديق قلبي). The servant’s forgetting of his Lord results in the Exalted forgetting him, as He said: **نَسُوا اللهَ فَنَسِيَهُمْ** "They forgot Allah, so He forgot them" (9:67). This is the manifest loss (الخسران المبين). Regarding the description of the hypocrites (المنافقين), the Exalted said: **أُولئِكَ الَّذِينَ اشْتَرَوُا الضَّلالَةَ بِالْهُدى فَما رَبِحَتْ تِجارَتُهُمْ** "They are the ones who bought error for guidance, so their trade did not profit them" (2:16).

This is what is alluded to in the conclusion of the verse: **وَمَنْ يَفْعَلْ ذلِكَ فَأُولئِكَ هُمُ الْخاسِرُونَ** "and whoever does that—it is they who are the losers" (63:9).

The underlying intent is to forbid the believers from being diverted by wealth and children. However, the phrasing was shifted from forbidding the believers to forbidding the wealth and children from distracting them, to signal that it is in their very nature to distract. Therefore, it is not fitting for the believers to become attached to them such that they are distracted from the remembrance of Allah, the Glorified. This is a metonymical prohibition (نهي كنائي) that is more emphatic than a direct one.

**His saying, the Exalted:** **وَأَنْفِقُوا مِنْ ما رَزَقْناكُمْ مِنْ قَبْلِ أَنْ يَأْتِيَ أَحَدَكُمُ الْمَوْتُ** "And spend out of what We have provided you before death comes to any of you" (63:10) etc. This is a command to spend in righteousness (البر), encompassing both obligatory spending (الإنفاق الواجب)—such as the alms-tax (الزكاة) and expiations (الكفارات)—and recommended spending (المندوب). Restricting it with the phrase "out of what We have provided you" (مِنْ ما رَزَقْناكُمْ) is to intimate that His command is not a request for something they own independently of Him. Rather, it is something He has granted them, a provision (رزق) He has provided, and a possession He has enabled them to own without it ever leaving His ultimate ownership. He commands them to spend a portion of it on what He desires; thus, the favor (المنة) belongs to Him over them in every state.

His saying: **مِنْ قَبْلِ أَنْ يَأْتِيَ أَحَدَكُمُ الْمَوْتُ** "before death comes to any of you" means before the period of one's ability to dispose of his wealth by spending in the way of Allah is cut short.

His saying: **فَيَقُولَ رَبِّ لَوْ لا أَخَّرْتَنِي إِلى أَجَلٍ قَرِيبٍ** "and he says, ‘My Lord, why did You not respite me for a short term’" (63:10) is conjoined to "comes" (أَنْ يَأْتِيَ). The term (الأجل) is described as "short" (قريب) to indicate that he is content with even a small extension—the amount of time sufficient to spend—so that his request might be more easily granted. Furthermore, any term, no matter what it may be, is "near" (قريب). Among the sayings of the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم is: "Everything that is coming is near."

His saying: **فَأَصَّدَّقَ وَأَكُنْ مِنَ الصَّالِحِينَ** "so that I might give charity and become one of the righteous" (63:10). The verb "I might give charity" (فَأَصَّدَّقَ) is in the subjunctive because it is the response to a wish (تمني), while "I become" (أَكُنْ) is in the jussive because it carries the meaning of the recompense of a conditional statement (جزاء الشرط). The implied meaning is: "If I give charity, I shall be among the righteous."

**His saying, the Exalted:** **وَلَنْ يُؤَخِّرَ اللهُ نَفْساً إِذا جاءَ أَجَلُها** "But Allah will never respite a soul when its term has come" (63:11). This is to make them despair of having the prayer answered for those who ask for a delay of the term after it has arrived, and for death after it has descended and the signs of the Hereafter have appeared. It has been repeated in the speech of the Exalted that the "appointed term" (الأجل المسمى) is among the instances of the "decreed judgment" (القضاء المحتوم), as in His saying: **إِذا جاءَ أَجَلُهُمْ فَلا يَسْتَأْخِرُونَ ساعَةً وَلا يَسْتَقْدِمُونَ** "When their time comes, they shall not defer it by a single hour nor shall they advance it" (10:49).

His saying: **وَاللهُ خَبِيرٌ بِما تَعْمَلُونَ** "and Allah is well aware of what you do" (63:11) is either a circumstantial clause (حال) referring to the pronoun in "any of you" (أَحَدَكُمُ), or it is conjoined to the beginning of the discourse and serves as a justification (تعليل). The meaning is: Do not be distracted and do spend, for Allah is All-Knowing of your deeds and will recompense you for them.

## (Narrative Discussion)

In *al-Faqih*, it is narrated that he was asked about the saying of Allah, the Exalted: **فَأَصَّدَّقَ وَأَكُنْ مِنَ الصَّالِحِينَ** "so that I might give charity and become one of the righteous." He said: "**فَأَصَّدَّقَ** (so that I might give charity) refers to charity (الصدقة), and **أَكُنْ مِنَ الصَّالِحِينَ** (and become one of the righteous) means to perform the Hajj (أحج)."

**I say:** It is apparent that the end of the tradition is by way of pointing out some of the instances (المصاديق).

In *al-Majma'*, narrated from Ibn Abbas, who said: "There is no one who dies having possessed wealth but did not pay its alms-tax (زكاته)—or was able to perform the Hajj but did not—except that he asks for a return at the time of death."

They said: "O Ibn Abbas, fear Allah! We only see the disbeliever (الكافر) asking for a return." He replied: "I shall recite a Quranic verse to you regarding this," then he recited this verse—meaning His saying: **يا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا لا تُلْهِكُمْ** (O you who have faith! Do not let [your possessions] distract you) until His saying: **مِنَ الصَّالِحِينَ** (one of the righteous). He said: "Righteousness (الصلاح) here refers to the Hajj." This has also been narrated from Abu Abdillah عليه السلام.

**I say:** It was also narrated in *al-Durr al-Manthur* from several compilers of traditions on the authority of Ibn Abbas.

In *Tafsir al-Qummi*, with its chain of narration to Abu Basir from Abu Ja'far عليه السلام regarding the saying of Allah: **وَلَنْ يُؤَخِّرَ اللهُ نَفْساً إِذا جاءَ أَجَلُها** "But Allah will never respite a soul when its term has come." He said: "Indeed, with Allah there are suspended books (كتب موقوفة) from which He advances what He wills and delays what He wills. When it is the Night of Ordainment (ليلة القدر), Allah sends down therein everything that will occur until the next one. That is His saying: **وَلَنْ يُؤَخِّرَ اللهُ نَفْساً إِذا جاءَ أَجَلُها** (But Allah will never respite a soul when its term has come)—once Allah has sent it down and it is written in the books of the heavens; that is what is never delayed."

***
